data.body:
Severity: security low + performance medium. Source: TYB-12-followup audits (both flagged).

Where: server/internal/render/render.go:91-116.

What: RenderPNG takes r.mu.Lock() and calls r.r.RenderWithSize(svg, ...) with no context plumbed in. resvg-go is wasm and serialised through the mutex by design. There's no per-call timeout.

Impact: with body capped at 4 KiB and text capped at 10 runes (Validate), legitimate input is bounded — resvg should never hang. But a resvg bug or wasm-runtime stall would wedge every concurrent render until the inbound HTTP WriteTimeout (60s) cancels the request. The mutex is held by the goroutine that's still running inside the wasm call; canceling the inbound request does NOT unstick it. Health endpoint stays up; render path silently dies.

Recommendation: run the wasm call in a goroutine, select on ctx.Done() with a hard 10-15s budget; on timeout, return an error AND release the mutex (the leaked goroutine is acceptable — better than wedging). Optional: a watchdog that recreates the resvg context on repeated panic/timeout.

Pairs naturally with TYB-16 (renderer pool) — a pool needs cancellation semantics anyway. Recommend implementing both together.

Out of scope: full ctx propagation into resvg-go (requires upstream library support).
